claim: Picking terminal + WarehousePro WMS as the main tool
quote: here's my terminal, I scan the bin barcode, then the item barcode

claim: Environment artifacts: notebook and stickers on the monitor
quote: on the desk a battered grid-paper notebook and sticky notes on the monitor

claim: Opening the picking task for order 48213
quote: I'm opening the picking task, here, order number 48213, three line items

claim: Scanning bin A-12-3
quote: the respondent scans bin A-12-3, the terminal screen shows a green checkmark

claim: Closing the box and sticker after picking three items
quote: Yes, three items picked, I'm closing the box, sticking on a label with the order number, taking it to packing

claim: Workaround 1: hand-drawn warehouse map over system bin addresses
quote: here's my own map of the warehouse, I drew it myself two years ago and touch it up in pencil whenever stock gets moved around

claim: Workaround 2: sticky-note reminder about the terminal freezing
quote: don't scan the box twice!! froze on 3/14

claim: Workaround 3: own handwritten sticker over the box barcode
quote: the printer prints it so small and crooked that the packers sometimes mix up orders

claim: Think-aloud commentary triggered by the forcing question 'what are you doing right now'
quote: I'm opening the picking task, here, order number 48213, three line items

claim: Think-aloud commentary on skipping the bin scan in the fast-pick zone
quote: the fast-pick zone doesn't require scanning the bin, it's just open shelves there, I grab it and scan the item right away instead of the bin, it's faster that way

claim: Says: strictly by the system, no improvising
quote: Normally I do everything strictly by the system, it tells me the route through the warehouse, I walk it and pick, no improvising

claim: Does: walks toward B-7 before the terminal shows a route
quote: without waiting for the terminal to display the route, the respondent immediately turns toward rack B-7

claim: Says: strictly follows the order route without getting distracted
quote: Normally I try to stick strictly to the order route and not get distracted, we're strict about that

claim: Does: was sidetracked twice into unrelated actions outside the route
quote: the respondent was twice sidetracked into unrelated actions outside the order route

claim: Interruption: coworker's voice message in the shift chat
quote: a coworker replies in the chat with a voice message, the respondent listens through an earbud, nods

claim: Interruption: coworker approaches twice with a question about a neighboring rack
quote: a coworker approached the respondent twice with a question about a neighboring rack, the respondent answered briefly and kept picking

claim: Shift group chat as a collective resource for locating items
quote: someone on shift usually knows where the item actually is, and they tip me off quickly

claim: Coworker Sasha personally comes over for help locating parts
quote: Sasha just came by to ask where to find parts for a Daewoo, it's like that here all the time, we cover for each other

claim: Supervisor as a figure of social control not discussed openly
quote: I'm not sure he'd officially approve of it, we just don't talk about it out loud

claim: PROCESS-FAILURE WORKAROUND: notebook compensates for mismatched bin addresses
quote: in the system the bin addresses periodically don't match what's really on the shelf

claim: SYSTEM-FAILURE WORKAROUND: sticky note compensates for a known terminal freeze bug
quote: if you scan the same box twice in a row, the terminal freezes

claim: Ambiguous: box sticker is an entrenched shift habit, not just a personal fix
quote: all of us senior pickers do this, I didn't come up with it alone, it just sort of caught on about three years ago

claim: The route-related gap was stated confidently right before observation caught the opposite — looks unconscious
quote: no improvising

claim: The bin-scanning gap is a conscious adjustment for the supervisor/authority
quote: with the supervisor around I'd probably scan the bin just to keep things proper, but without him not always

claim: Respondent herself assesses fragility: without the notebook and stickers, speed would drop
quote: if you took my notebook away and stripped off the stickers, I'd probably work way slower for the first week, until I relearned where everything is

claim: Observation confirms reliance on the notebook rather than the instructions
quote: the respondent consulted the notebook seven times and the monitor stickers twice, never once opened the official picking instructions

claim: Official discrepancy report is too slow relative to the pace of work
quote: Through the report that's about a two-hour wait, through the chat it's usually five minutes

claim: The no-exceptions scanning procedure doesn't account for the fast-pick zone
quote: here in the fast-pick zone we all do it this way, otherwise there's no speed at all, you can't hit the quota

claim: Core insight: WMS records chronically lag reality, stability rests on personal artifacts
quote: we find about ten of these discrepancies per shift

claim: Respondent directly names the observer effect (Hawthorne)
quote: I'm working more carefully than usual today, because you're standing right here

claim: Example of normally hidden behavior given retrospectively, not shown live
quote: sometimes I pick two orders at once in parallel, grab boxes for both at the same time so I don't have to make two trips, even though the instructions say strictly one order at a time

claim: Observation notes the absence of a second box at the time of the account
quote: the respondent is standing with one box in hand, no second box visible nearby
